location.office_time_required: flexible; regular in-person events encouraged under Flex First policy

level_signals: 5+ years of work experience post-graduation in a technology field
Base pay range $178K–$226K depending on location, consistent with Senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of work experience post-graduation in a technology field, preferably as a technical program or project manager, product manager, software engineer (technical domain), or related technical discipline.

Demonstrated ability to create functional specs, set program structure and influence program strategy. Previous experience in managing complex multi-org programs end-to-end, from definition to launch, throughout a software development lifecycle.
A strong communicator with the ability to distill multiple streams of information, convey priorities and risks, and build a narrative for stakeholders.
A strong sense of ownership and intellectual curiosity to understand executional and technical problems in depth, identify potential issues and offer actions to prevent them.
Excellent collaborator and motivator, able to bring a team together to align and deliver on a common goal. Can build trust and partnerships with senior Engineering and Product leaders.
Growth mindset - seeks and is receptive to actionable feedback and growth opportunities.
Takes accountability seriously; can operate independently to deliver responsibilities and milestones on time and within budget.
Self-motivated and thrives in a fast-paced environment.
Bachelor's degree in a technology / engineering discipline.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Master's degree in a technology/engineering discipline.

Demonstrated ability to showcase deep technical domain knowledge on the programs that you take on, enabling you to be seen as a strong technical voice in the program and adding significant value towards architecting a program.
Demonstrated ability to drive architectural and strategic decisions at the org leadership level.
Extensive previous experience around creating innovative program management processes/tools/approaches to deliver company outcomes reliably and efficiently.
Experience working with e-commerce solutions at scale.

scope.surface: Enterprise platform — retailer onboarding, transitions, and growth across Instacart's multi-sided marketplace
scope.business_model: B2B2C marketplace (grocery delivery connecting customers, shoppers, brands, and retailer partners)
primary_metrics_inferred: Program delivery against milestones and budget
Operational excellence of retailer launch framework
core_responsibilities: Lead planning and high quality technical execution of large engineering programs with high ambiguity, complexity, and visibility
Work with cross-functional partners to set project objectives and metric-based success criteria
Own clear and effective communication with stakeholders on priorities, risks, and changes
Foster a culture of teamwork and shared goals within the program team
Uphold and continuously contribute to the operational excellence framework of retailer launches

compensation.equity_form: New hire equity grant plus annual refresh grants

green_flags: Detailed compensation ranges disclosed across multiple US geographies
Equity explicitly mentioned (new hire + annual refresh)
Remote-first (Flex First) policy with flexible work location
Clear program scope around Enterprise retailer platform
Role embedded within Engineering org — strong technical credibility signal
red_flags: No visa sponsorship information stated — unclear for international candidates
Wide compensation band across states ($178K–$226K base) may signal significant geographic pay differences
Fast-paced, high-ambiguity environment described — could indicate resource or organizational churn
Bachelor's degree in technology/engineering listed as hard requirement with no equivalent experience alternative stated

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Senior' and comp range)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description of multi-sided marketplace)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly stated)
compensation.base_range_usd reported as min of lowest band and max of highest band across all US geographies
ambiguities: The JD describes a 'Flex First' policy allowing employees to choose where they work, suggesting remote-global eligibility is possible, but all compensation bands listed are US-specific; treated as remote-us.

Bachelor's degree listed under Minimum Qualifications with no 'or equivalent experience' caveat, making it a hard requirement; however the JD does not explicitly state it is eliminatory — flagged as hard severity based on placement under Minimum Qualifications.
